verdict: Spend collapsed to $5.34 on the latest day from a $20/day average — campaign appears to be stalling out.
diagnosis: Facebook lead campaign spent $1,324 over 28 days but daily pacing dropped 74% recently, with no lead or conversion data visible to judge efficiency.

signals.headline: Total spend hit $1,324 this period
signals.detail: One active Facebook lead campaign carried all paid investment. No diversification across channels or campaign types this period.

signals.headline: Daily spend crashed to $5.34
signals.detail: Latest day ran 74% below the $20.37 daily average. Suggests budget exhaustion, delivery issues, or manual pause.

signals.headline: Zero visibility on 0 leads
signals.detail: No lead volume, cost per lead, or pipeline data connected. Impossible to confirm if spend is producing members.

signals.headline: Only 1 campaign running
signals.detail: Single interest-based lead campaign means no creative or audience testing. Performance ceiling is locked in.

signals.headline: No organic or local data tracked
signals.detail: Missing Google Business Profile, social, and CRM feeds. Paid is flying without context on brand demand or close rates.

benchmark.label: Benchmark
benchmark.body: Facebook lead ads for fitness average $29.70 per lead in 2026. Budget gyms run $8–$15, mid-range $15–$30, boutique studios $25–$50. Without lead tracking, Vous Physique cannot tell where it lands.

seasonal.label: Seasonal
seasonal.body: Fitness lead costs run lowest in spring, with April historically the year's trough near $13.50. Costs spike sharply in July. The current window is favorable for scaling, not pulling back.

competitor.label: Competitor
competitor.subheader: Studios shifting to retention and community
competitor.body: Boutique studios are pivoting from acquisition to retention, community building, and Pilates or hybrid formats. Class attendance is climbing past 2019 levels and average prices rose 6% year over year.

recommendations.title: Connect lead tracking before spending another dollar
recommendations.detail: Wire Facebook lead form submissions into a CRM or spreadsheet. Without cost per lead visibility, the $1,324 spent this period cannot be judged as good or bad.

recommendations.title: Investigate why daily spend dropped to $5.34
recommendations.detail: Check if the campaign hit budget caps, lost delivery, or was paused. Q2 is the cheapest season for fitness leads — worth restoring pacing if performance was working.

recommendations.title: Test a second campaign angle alongside interests
recommendations.detail: Consider adding a lookalike or retargeting campaign. Running one interest-based campaign limits learning. Community and Pilates angles are trending in the category right now.
